encounter.location: UNC Medical Center, 101 Manning Drive, Chapel Hill, NC 27514

conditions.name: peanut allergy with history of anaphylaxis

medications.name: fluticasone/salmeterol (Advair HFA 115/21 mcg) inhaler
medications.sig: 1 puff inhaled twice daily via spacer

medications.name: albuterol (ProAir HFA 90 mcg) inhaler
medications.sig: 2 puffs inhaled PRN for wheezing or shortness of breath via spacer

medications.name: epinephrine auto-injector (EpiPen Jr 0.15 mg) two-pack
medications.sig: carried at all times, PRN for anaphylaxis
medications.reason: peanut allergy with history of anaphylaxis

medications.name: triamcinolone 0.1% ointment
medications.sig: apply to eczema flares twice daily as needed

document_references.description: Routine asthma and allergy follow-up via synchronous audio-video telemedicine. Documents subjective report from mother regarding asthma control (improved but not fully controlled with weekly rescue inhaler use for exercise-induced symptoms), food allergy status (no accidental exposures, tolerating baked egg), eczema and rhinitis status (well-controlled). Assessment of five active conditions with plan to continue current regimen and schedule in-person visit February 2026 for FeNO, spirometry, and specific IgE retesting. Notes age eligibility for omalizumab at age 6 in March 2026 if needed.
